key_insight: Mesh-RL speeds up reinforcement learning by decomposing the environment into overlapping subgrids and enforcing boundary-consistent value updates so rewards propagate more globally.
engineering_relevance: The method imports finite-element/domain-decomposition ideas into RL, offering a principled way to improve sample efficiency in sparse-reward, large-state environments.
method: Partition the grid environment into overlapping subgrids.
Apply localized temporal-difference updates with boundary consistency across subgrids.
Use the decomposition with standard RL algorithms such as Q-learning, SARSA, and Dyna-Q.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on hazard-dense grid worlds with different geometries and mesh resolutions, Mesh-RL consistently improved convergence speed, cumulative reward, and learning stability across Q-learning, SARSA, and Dyna-Q.
watch_item: Performance may depend on choosing an appropriate mesh resolution and overlap; overly coarse or poorly tuned decomposition could reduce the benefit.
